articles.title: Explained: Why oil prices aren't rising despite fresh attacks near the Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Fresh security incidents near the Strait of Hormuz have failed to trigger a surge in oil prices because crude supplies remain plentiful, shipping continues largely uninterrupted and traders see little risk of a prolonged disruption—for now.

articles.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures edge lower as chip stocks retreat; crude oil prices rise
articles.description: US stock futures indicate a weak start as AI stocks decline following concerns over Samsung's earnings. S&P 500 futures dropped 0.2%, while Nasdaq fell 0.8%. Investor focus shifts to upcoming earnings from Delta and PepsiCo amid rising US dollar and Treasury yields.

articles.title: Rupee rises 48 paise to close at 94.95 against U.S. dollar
articles.description: Rupee rises 48 paise to 94.95 against the dollar, buoyed by lower crude oil prices and improved global risk sentiment.

articles.title: Turbulence in the Strait: Tanker Strikes Amid Khamenei Mourning
articles.description: In the Strait of Hormuz, two tankers, including an LNG carrier at explosion risk, were hit while mourning for Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ensued. The attacks underscore Gulf shipping insecurity and highlight regional power struggles amid U.S.-Iran tensions. Oil prices have since risen slightly.

articles.title: E20 can damage rubber parts in older cars, says ARAI report that was never published
articles.description: E20 Petrol Damage Engine: The use of E20 petrol in E10-compatible vehicles can damage fuel-system rubber parts, found a study by the Automotive Research Association of India (ARAI). The report, which remains unpublished, however, backed the use of E20 fuel. The findings align with the concerns expressed by owners of pre-2023 vehicles.

articles.title: Woman Pours Petrol On Herself During Public Hearing In MP’s Shivpuri; Alleges Police Inaction Over Dowry Harassment - VIDEO
articles.description: A woman created a stir at Shivpuri Police Control Room by threatening self-harm with a petrol bottle, alleging dowry harassment, assault, and attempts to evict her from her matrimonial home. She claimed police ignored earlier complaints. Her husband also alleged inaction by authorities. Police intervened, counseled the woman, seized the bottle, and launched an investigation.
